option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"/Users/randym/Documents/GitHub/portfolio":"{\"/css/search.css\":\"This file contains CSS styles for a search bar component. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the styles for the search bar and its various elements. Here\\u0027s a summary of the file:\\n\\n`.search-bar`: Defines the overall styles for the search bar container, including its size, positioning, and visual appearance.\\n\\n`.search-inner`: Styles the inner container of the search bar, which holds the search input and any additional elements.\\n\\n`.search-inner input[type\\u003d\\\"search\\\"]`: Styles the search input field, including its appearance, behavior, and interactions.\\n\\n`.search-stats`: Styles the search statistics or additional information displayed alongside the search input.\\n\\n`.search-dim`: Applies a visual filtering effect to the search bar when it is in a \\\"dimmed\\\" state, such as when the user is not interacting with it.\\n\\n`.visually-hidden`: Defines a class for visually hiding an element while keeping it accessible to screen readers and other assistive technologies.\",\"/css/gallery.css\":\"This file is a CSS stylesheet that is scoped to the gallery page of a website. It is designed to be loaded after the main `style.css` file, so that it can override certain rules where needed. The file contains styles for the layout, controls, gallery grid, load more/pagination, focus styles, and responsive tweaks. It also includes some overrides for the search bar and modal functionality. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a set of CSS rules that define the appearance and behavior of the gallery page.\",\"/css/hover.css\":\"This file contains CSS styles for an interactive artwork carousel. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the styles for the carousel images and their hover/active states. Here\\u0027s a summary of the styles:\\n\\n`.carousel-track img`: Defines the base transition and transformation properties for the carousel images.\\n`.img-hover-lift`: Applies a lifted state to the carousel images when they are hovered over or interacted with.\\n`.img-hover-active`: Applies a slightly snappier transition response for the active carousel image.\\n`@media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)`: Provides a fallback hover effect for devices that support hover interactions.\\n`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`: Ensures that the animations and transitions are disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the visual and interactive behavior of an image carousel, with smooth transitions and hover effects to enhance the user experience.\",\"/js/gallery.js\":\"This file is a JavaScript code for a gallery application. It handles the rendering of thumbnails, filtering, searching, and displaying a modal view for individual images. Here are the key functions and their summaries:\\n\\n`initLazyObserver`: Initializes a lazy loading observer to load images only when they are visible on the page.\\n\\n`normalizeData`: Normalizes the data structure of the gallery items.\\n\\n`scanCarouselImages`: Scans the carousel images on the page and returns their data.\\n\\n`slugify`: Generates a unique slug-like identifier for an item.\\n\\n`renderPage`: Renders a page of thumbnails based on the current state of the application.\\n\\n`makeThumb`: Creates a thumbnail element for a gallery item.\\n\\n`bindControls`: Binds event listeners to various UI controls.\\n\\n`applyFilters`: Applies the current filters and search query to the gallery items.\\n\\n`openModalForItem`: Opens the modal view for a specific gallery item.\\n\\n`showCurrent`: Displays the current item in the modal view.\\n\\n`showRelative`: Navigates to the previous or next item in the modal view.\\n\\n`performGalleryTransition`: Performs a smooth transition when navigating between items in the modal view.\\n\\n`updateNavButtons`: Updates the state of the previous and next buttons in the modal view.\\n\\n`closeModal`: Closes the modal view.\\n\\n`debounce`: A utility function to debounce input events.\\n\\n`handleInitialHash`: Handles the initial hash in the URL to open the corresponding item in the modal view.\",\"/gallery.json\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be a JSON document that describes a collection of fan art images, including their titles, descriptions, dates, and the collections they belong to. The file provides detailed information about each piece of fan art, such as the characters depicted, the media used, and the artistic style.\",\"/css/style.css\":\"This file appears to be a CSS stylesheet that defines the styles for a website or web application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a set of styles that are applied to various HTML elements. The main sections of the stylesheet are:\\n\\n1. **Base/Reset**: Resets the default styles for HTML elements and sets up a consistent baseline.\\n2. **Layout: Fixed Sidebar + Scrollable Main**: Defines the layout of the page, with a fixed sidebar on the left and a scrollable main content area on the right.\\n3. **About Section Enhancements**: Styles the \\\"About\\\" section of the page, including a grid layout for the signature and text.\\n4. **Sidebar separators (fixed-position, non-layout)**: Adds fixed-position separators within the sidebar.\\n5. **Carousel Styles**: Styles the carousel component used to display images.\\n6. **Arrow shells positioned at the TOP-RIGHT of the .glass panel**: Adds arrow buttons to the carousel.\\n7. **Modal/Lightbox**: Styles the modal/lightbox component used to display larger images.\\n8. **Responsive adjustments**: Includes media queries to adjust the layout and styles for smaller screens.\\n9. **Additional styles for Mario GIF and Instagram icon**: Adds styles for a Mario GIF and an Instagram icon in the sidebar.\\n\\nThe file appears to be a comprehensive CSS stylesheet that defines the visual styles and layout for a website or web application, with a focus on creating a visually appealing and responsive design.\",\"/js/hover.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a JavaScript code that adds hover and tilt effects to carousel images on a web page. The code uses feature detection to ensure that the effects only run on devices that support hover and pointer interactions, and it also respects the user\\u0027s preference for reduced motion. The code applies the hover and tilt effects to all images inside the carousel tracks, and it uses requestAnimationFrame to throttle the transform updates for smooth animations.\",\"/js/search.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a JavaScript code that implements a live search functionality for a carousel-based user interface. The code performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Debounces the input event to prevent excessive search queries.\\n2. Normalizes the search query to make it case-insensitive.\\n3. Iterates through the carousel tracks, identifies matching items based on the search query, and rearranges the items in the carousel accordingly.\\n4. Dispatches a custom event to notify the carousel of the reordering.\\n5. Displays the number of search results.\\n6. Scrolls the carousel to the first track with matching items when the user presses the \\\"Enter\\\" key.\\n7. Resets the carousel to its original state when the user clears the search input or presses the \\\"Escape\\\" key.\",\"/index.html\":\"This file appears to be an HTML document that serves as an artist portfolio website for Randy Mattoka, a digital illustrator and concept artist. The file includes several CSS files (`style.css`, `search.css`, and `hover.css`) and three JavaScript files (`search.js`, `script.js`, and `hover.js`). The HTML structure includes a fixed left sidebar, a main content area, and a modal/lightbox for displaying artwork details. The file does not contain any explicitly defined classes or functions, but the JavaScript files likely contain functions to handle the interactive features of the website, such as the search functionality, carousel, and modal/lightbox.\",\"/js/script.js\":\"This file contains a carousel script and a lightbox/magnifier functionality. It does not contain any classes, but it has the following functions:\\n\\n`slideSize`: Calculates the size of each slide in the carousel.\\n`update`: Updates the carousel\\u0027s position and state based on the current index.\\n`showLens`: Shows the magnifier lens.\\n`hideLens`: Hides the magnifier lens.\\n`syncCarousel`: Synchronizes the background carousel to the image selected in the modal.\\n`performImageTransition`: Performs a smooth transition between images in the modal.\\n`updateModalContent`: Updates the content of the modal with the new image and its details.\\n`openModal`: Opens the modal and sets up the necessary event listeners.\\n`showPrev`: Shows the previous image in the modal.\\n`showNext`: Shows the next image in the modal.\\n`handleKeyNavigation`: Handles keyboard navigation in the modal.\\n`updateMagnifierSize`: Updates the size of the magnifier lens.\\n`handleMagnify`: Handles the magnification of the image in the modal.\\n`closeLightbox`: Closes the modal and removes the event listeners.\\n\\nThe file also includes some additional logic for handling keyboard focus and sidebar navigation.\",\"/gallery.html\":\"This file appears to be an HTML document that represents a gallery page for an artist named Randy Mattoka. It includes a sidebar with navigation, a search bar, a gallery grid, and a modal overlay for displaying individual artworks. The file also includes references to several CSS and JavaScript files that provide the functionality for the gallery.\\n\\n`sidebar`: Defines the structure and content of the sidebar, including the artist\\u0027s name, navigation links, and social media links.\\n\\n`search-bar`: Provides a search functionality for the gallery, allowing users to search for artworks by title, description, or other criteria.\\n\\n`gallery-grid`: Displays a grid of artworks, with the ability to filter the grid by different categories (e.g., completed, Nightwatch, process).\\n\\n`image-modal`: Displays a modal overlay with a magnified view of the selected artwork, along with its title, description, and metadata.\\n\\n`script.js`: Likely contains the JavaScript code that powers the gallery functionality, such as loading and filtering the artworks, handling user interactions, and managing the modal overlay.\\n\\n`hover.js`: Likely contains the JavaScript code that handles the hover effects for the artworks in the gallery grid.\\n\\n`gallery.js`: Likely contains the JavaScript code that manages the gallery-specific functionality, such as loading and displaying the artworks, handling the search and filtering functionality, and managing the modal overlay.\",\"/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be a simple HTML document that serves as an artist portfolio, with a top-level heading \\\"Portfolio\\\" and a brief description \\\"This is my artist portfolio\\\".\"}"}
